items.quote: No confirmed executive economic buyer or champion has joined a call; budget authority beyond 'centrally held' is unmapped.

items.source: No read source through 7 Jul 2026 quantifies financial impact

items.quote: Provide details on recent S/4HANA integrations (including any Saudi implementations) and confirm iSaaS adapter capabilities.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is clearly articulated (fragmented, manual supplier-facing processes across onboarding, qualification, sourcing and development; lack of transparency and standardized supplier data across HOLDCO/OPCOs; need for one auditable platform supporting direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP) per MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 15 May 2026). No quantified financial impact or ROI figure has surfaced in either read call (13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026) or in the Next Step log through 7 Jul 2026.

champion.narrative: No internal champion exists. MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026) states plainly: 'Very arms-length process - Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role.' No SAMI stakeholder has been observed advocating for Zycus internally across either read call.

competition.narrative: Four named rivals are tracked: SAP Ariba (SAP/IBM ecosystem relationship, disliked by the buyer per MEDDPICC but a real S/4HANA-integration risk given SAMI's live S/4HANA rollout), Ivalua (heavy customization/localization fit), GEP Worldwide (heavy services-support model, potentially appealing for a complex defense buyer), and Coupa (strong Aerospace & Defense customer base, directly relevant to SAMI's sector). All were named as of the MEDDPICC 2.0 update (15 May 2026); no fresher named-competitor evidence appears in the 23 Jun 2026 demo notes.

identify_pain.narrative: SAMI is undertaking a parallel SAP S/4HANA ERP upgrade and needs procurement modernized alongside it; Franck Devienne described this directly on the 13 Jan 2026 call ('we are all looking together right now into setting up the new ERP based on SAP... the part of the functionality we will build up... let's call it the extended design as described in the RFI'). MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026) frames this as fragmented, manual, non-standardized supplier processes across HOLDCO/OPCOs needing one auditable platform.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or formal contracting artifact has entered the record — the deal is still in the RFP/evaluation phase at Shortlisted, not yet Vendor Selected. The only paper-adjacent item is the requirement to route revised commercial quotes through SI partners KPMG and Accenture, per the 3 Jul 2026 Next Step, which is itself unresolved.

economic_buyer.narrative: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor) is named as Economic Buyer in MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026), and budget is described as 'centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' However, no CFO/CPO-level executive with real budget authority has joined either read call (13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026), and MEDDPICC 2.0 itself notes the relationship with Franck is 'very arms-length.'

decision_process.narrative: The process is a clear, dated sequence: RFI (NDA 15 Dec 2025, RFI received 16 Dec 2025, deadline 24 Jan 2026) → Innovation Contest presentations (22-26 Feb 2026 window, delivered ~24-26 Feb 2026) → SAMI confidentially confirmed shortlisting to three vendors (2 Mar 2026) → RFP issued (13 Mar 2026, extended to 21 Apr 2026) → RFP submitted with extensive clarification rounds (4 May, 13 May, 21 May, 6 Jun 2026) → live SRM demo (23 Jun 2026) → SAMI-stated final round (~mid-July 2026) and evaluation conclusion (end of July 2026). Traceable across nine dated Next Step History entries.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are granular and well documented: functional/technical compliance, cybersecurity compliance, SAP/S/4HANA integration, A&D/direct-procurement/MRO fit, local content/localization/LCGPA/SDP/IPP fit, KSA implementation/support capability, knowledge transfer, implementation plan, a filled compliance matrix, and commercial response (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026). Corroborated live in the 23 Jun 2026 demo, where the SAMI A&D technical reviewer probed S/4HANA integration, local-content dashboards, and historical-price-view functionality in detail.

items.name: Yousef chases overdue SI proposals
items.summary: Yousef (SAMI) followed up on Franck's June 22 request, noting the vendor proposals and AEC hosting info were promised 'early this week' but still hadn't arrived by Thursday — a pointed nudge signaling SAMI's patience is wearing thin.

items.name: Carl's status update on SI partners + revised DC slide
items.summary: Carl replied same-day with the AEC data warehouse slide update and a partner-by-partner status: EY nearly done reconciling teams, KPMG committing to finish in 1-2 days, and Accenture again declining to bid alone (wants to pair with another ecosystem partner SAMI hasn't approved); final implementation commercials are on hold until all proposals and joint costs are finalized.

day_summary.overall: SAMI's Yousef chased Zycus (again) for the overdue SI vendor proposals and AEC hosting details, having expected them 'early this week' but still empty-handed by Thursday. Carl finally responded same-day with a substantive status update: EY's proposal is nearly finalized, KPMG has committed to finishing in 1-2 days, but Accenture has again refused to bid solo (insisting on partnering with another ecosystem vendor SAMI doesn't want). Carl also flagged that final implementation commercials can't be locked until all SI proposals land and joint costs are optimized — so the deal remains stalled on Zycus's side pending partner deliverables, with SAMI now visibly frustrated at the slipping timeline.

cro_panel.intro: SAMI_S2P is a partner-led, Saudi defense-and-aerospace S2P deal in the endgame of a formal RFP; SAMI confirmed a mid-July 2026 final round and an end-of-July 2026 evaluation conclusion.; Zycus was confidentially down-selected to a final shortlist of three (confirmed 2 Mar 2026) and remains in a live bake-off against SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP Worldwide and Coupa.; No confirmed champion or direct executive economic-buyer engagement exists above Franck Devienne, and defense-compliance questions raised at the 23 Jun 2026 demo remain unanswered; real gaps against an otherwise well-documented, multi-touch process

confidence_signals.summary: The deal shows real, sustained buyer-side process investment (RFI through RFP through Shortlist, live demos, a confirmed two-way email 2 days ago) but lacks executive access and a champion, which caps confidence in the win.

north_star_verdict.math: 52 days to close (2026-08-29) as of 8 Jul 2026. Deal has sat in Shortlisted since SAMI confirmed the down-select to 3 vendors on 2 Mar 2026 (~128 days in stage), moving through RFP submission (4 May 2026), ~600 clarifications (13 May 2026), and a live SRM demo (23 Jun 2026) that landed well on functional fit. SAMI's own stated timeline (23 Jun demo notes + 3 Jul 2026 Next Step) points to evaluation conclusion by end of July 2026 and a final decision round around mid-July 2026. Two Zycus-side deliverables (written defense-compliance responses; revised SI-partner quotes) have been outstanding since 23 Jun 2026 (~15 days as of today), stalling the run-up to that decision. If SAMI decides mid-to-late July, the 29 Aug 2026 close leaves only 4-6 weeks for SI-partner-mediated commercial negotiation, defense/InfoSec compliance close-out, and contracting on a $2.2M enterprise A&D deal.

north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline until SAMI's decision converts to a confirmed shortlist win (expected mid-to-late Jul 2026); if won, re-forecast close to Oct-Nov 2026 to allow for SI-partner commercial negotiation and defense/InfoSec compliance close-out.

flags: Close defense-compliance gap immediately — trade/FARS, classified-material lineage, and consignment/MRP integration answers are overdue (23 Jun action item) and SAMI expects written responses before mid-July decision; unanswered gaps hand Ariba the win on 'SAP ecosystem = safer choice'.

Secure EB access and build a champion — Franck is a gatekeeper, not the budget authority or an advocate; the CFO/CPO-level economic buyer is unmapped and has not engaged. Request Franck's introduction to the budget-holding exec and identify a senior procurement/transformation lead who values AI/Merlin differentiation to drive Zycus to preferred-vendor status before mid-July decision.

SAP Ariba's assumed S/4HANA/SAP-ecosystem integration edge is unanswered since the 23 Jun 2026 demo; supplying concrete Saudi/S/4HANA reference proof is the single highest-leverage move to close the credibility gap before evaluation concludes end of July 2026.

The MEDDPICC 2.0 blocker 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted' threatens Zycus's only real differentiator (Merlin/Agentic AI); validate an on-prem/private-cloud AI deployment model now so this doesn't default to disqualification before the decision window.
Convert Franck Devienne from arms-length gatekeeper to advocate by arming him with a defensible written business case, since MEDDPICC 2.0 already flags he 'doesn't fit the champion role.'

No champion exists inside SAMI beyond the arms-length gatekeeper Franck Devienne; with the award decision expected mid-July 2026, cultivate an internal advocate among the technical/A&D stakeholders who joined the 23 Jun demo.

Zycus is currently the bottleneck on its own committed SI implementation proposal (KPMG/EY), chased three times by the buyer since 16 Jun 2026; closing this fast protects the 'good' technical position already earned in the demo.

Close both overdue 23 Jun deliverables (defense-compliance written answers, revised SI-routed quotes) this week; SAMI's stated end-of-July evaluation close leaves little room for slow responses to reopen the file.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: SAMI_S2P is a partner-led, Saudi defense-and-aerospace S2P deal in the endgame of a formal RFP: SAMI confirmed a mid-July 2026 final round and an end-of-July 2026 evaluation conclusion. Zycus was confidentially down-selected to a final shortlist of three (confirmed 2 Mar 2026) and remains in a live bake-off against SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP Worldwide and Coupa. No confirmed champion or direct executive economic-buyer engagement exists above Franck Devienne, and defense-compliance questions raised at the 23 Jun 2026 demo remain unanswered — real gaps against an otherwise well-documented, multi-touch process.

deal_risk.text: AI data-residency restriction threatens Zycus's core differentiator — MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026) records a blocker that 'zero AI functionality will be accepted' under KSA cybersecurity and data-residency rules, which would strip Zycus's positioned AI edge over its rivals if on-prem AI is not validated as compliant.

Saudization and delivery-resource restrictions add execution risk — MEDDPICC 2.0 flags a requirement of at least 30% Saudi-national on-site staffing and restricted nationalities for delivery resources, on top of the unresolved defense-compliance questions.

Single-threaded on the buyer side — Franck Devienne is the only consistently engaged SAMI contact across nine months (13 Jan 2026 through 3 Jul 2026); other named contacts (Yousef Alarabi, Ahmed Almania) are logged but not shown driving the process in any read call.

win_position.text: Down-selected to a final shortlist of three — SAMI confidentially confirmed on 2 Mar 2026 that Zycus was shortlisted, ahead of the formal RFP issuance.

Decision criteria and process are fully mapped — MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026) details the RFI to Innovation Contest to RFP to evaluation to award sequence, corroborated across nine dated Next Step History entries from 16 Dec 2025 through 3 Jul 2026.
SAP Ariba is a structurally advantaged live rival — SAMI runs SAP S/4HANA and Zycus has not yet supplied the Saudi/S/4HANA integration proof requested at the 23 Jun 2026 demo.

No confirmed champion — MEDDPICC 2.0 (15 May 2026) states the process with Franck Devienne is 'very arms-length' and that he 'doesn't fit the champion role,' with no internal advocate surfaced across nine months of engagement.
Economic-buyer access is thin — no CFO/CPO-level executive with real budget authority has joined either read call (13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026), despite Franck Devienne being named EB in MEDDPICC 2.0.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer engagement two days ago — an incoming buyer email arrived 7 Jul 2026, following the rep's 3 Jul 2026 update confirming SAMI's evaluation timeline.

A live, tailored demo was delivered 23 Jun 2026 (about two and a half weeks ago), covering sourcing, supplier onboarding, local-content dashboards and S/4HANA integration, with detailed buyer-side technical questioning.
Two substantive deliverables are overdue — written defense-compliance responses and revised commercial quotes via KPMG/Accenture, both flagged 3 Jul 2026, remain unresolved as of 9 Jul 2026.

The recorded 29 Aug 2026 close date is not credible — SAMI itself named an end-of-July 2026 evaluation conclusion and a mid-July 2026 final round, leaving no realistic room for negotiation, InfoSec/legal review and signature in the remaining 51 days.
